summary: Hardcoded Gmail SMTP credentials route caller email through author's account
details: utils/send_email_otp.py hardcodes a Gmail address (magizhchisk@gmail.com) and a Gmail app password as the sender for an exported OTP-email helper. The function logs into smtp.gmail.com:587 with these credentials and calls server.send_message(msg) on caller-supplied message content; the sender account is not configurable through the API. Any consumer that calls this helper silently relays its outbound email (recipient addresses, message bodies, OTP codes) through the author's personal Gmail mailbox, where the author can read everything sent. Separately, the plaintext app password is shipped in the importable source, so any installer can extract and abuse the author's Gmail account directly. The silent-relay channel is the installer-facing harm: callers believe they are sending mail under their own identity, but every message is delivered from and visible to the author's account.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
